UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores user-specific profile information, distinct from authentication credentials handled by Firebase Auth.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification and linked to their Firebase Auth account.

fullName.description: The full name of the user.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture, if available.

preferredLanguage.description: The user's preferred language for app content, e.g., 'English' or 'Hindi'.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the user profile.

StudyMaterial.title: StudyMaterial
StudyMaterial.description: Represents a piece of study material ingested by the user for assessment generation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudyMaterial entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who uploaded or provided the study material. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N StudyMaterial)

title.description: A user-defined title for the study material.

contentUrl.description: URL to the uploaded file (PDF, PPT, Image) in cloud storage.

textContent.description: The raw text content if the material was pasted directly by the user.

language.description: The language of the study material, e.g., 'English' or 'Hindi'.

uploadDate.description: Timestamp when the study material was uploaded or created.

lastModifiedDate.description: Timestamp of the last modification to the study material.

Assessment.title: Assessment
Assessment.description: Defines a set of questions generated by AI from study material, based on user-specified parameters.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Assessment entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who requested the assessment generation. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Assessment)

studyMaterialId.description: Reference to the StudyMaterial from which the assessment was generated. (Relationship: StudyMaterial 1:N Assessment)

title.description: A descriptive title for the assessment, either user-provided or auto-generated.

academicLevel.description: The academic level targeted by the assessment, e.g., 'High School', 'College'.

difficulty.description: The difficulty level of the assessment, e.g., 'Easy', 'Medium', 'Hard'.

questionCount.description: The number of questions generated for this assessment.

generationParameters.description: A JSON string storing the specific parameters used by the AI model for generation.

generatedDate.description: Timestamp when the assessment was generated.

Question.title: Question
Question.description: Represents a single question or prompt within an assessment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Question entity.

assessmentId.description: Reference to the Assessment this question belongs to. (Relationship: Assessment 1:N Question)

questionText.description: The text of the question or prompt.

options.description: An array of possible answer options for MCQ type questions.

correctAnswer.description: The correct answer for MCQ or Flashcard questions. For Essay prompts, this might be null or an outline.

explanation.description: An explanation for the correct answer or further clarification.

topic.description: An automatically identified academic topic associated with the question, used for performance tracking.

UserAssessmentAttempt.title: UserAssessmentAttempt
UserAssessmentAttempt.description: Records a user's single attempt at completing an assessment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserAssessmentAttempt entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who made the attempt. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserAssessmentAttempt)

assessmentId.description: Reference to the Assessment that was attempted. (Relationship: Assessment 1:N UserAssessmentAttempt)

attemptDate.description: Timestamp when the assessment attempt was initiated or completed.

overallScore.description: The overall score obtained by the user for this assessment attempt (e.g., percentage).

status.description: The current status of the attempt, e.g., 'completed', 'in-progress', 'graded'.

durationSeconds.description: The total time, in seconds, the user spent on this assessment attempt.

UserQuestionResponse.title: UserQuestionResponse
UserQuestionResponse.description: Stores a user's response to a specific question within an assessment attempt.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserQuestionResponse entity.

userAssessmentAttemptId.description: Reference to the UserAssessmentAttempt this response belongs to. (Relationship: UserAssessmentAttempt 1:N UserQuestionResponse)

questionId.description: Reference to the Question to which the user responded. (Relationship: Question 1:N UserQuestionResponse)

userAnswer.description: The user's submitted answer (e.g., selected option ID, typed text for essay/flashcard).

isCorrect.description: Indicates if the user's answer was correct (for auto-gradable questions).

score.description: The score obtained for this specific question, especially relevant for essay or partially correct answers.

feedback.description: Specific feedback provided for this question's response by the system or AI.

responseTimeSeconds.description: The time, in seconds, taken by the user to answer this question.

answeredAt.description: Timestamp when the user submitted this specific answer.

SubmittedEssay.title: SubmittedEssay
SubmittedEssay.description: Stores an essay submitted by a user for AI evaluation, potentially outside of a full assessment attempt.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SubmittedEssay entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who submitted the essay. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N SubmittedEssay)

questionId.description: Reference to the Question entity representing the essay prompt. (Relationship: Question 1:N SubmittedEssay)

submittedText.description: The full text of the essay submitted by the user (typed or OCR'd).

submissionDate.description: Timestamp when the essay was submitted for evaluation.

EssayEvaluation.title: EssayEvaluation
EssayEvaluation.description: Stores the comprehensive evaluation and feedback provided by the AI for a submitted essay.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EssayEvaluation entity.

submittedEssayId.description: Reference to the SubmittedEssay that was evaluated. (Relationship: SubmittedEssay 1:N EssayEvaluation)

overallScore.description: The overall numerical score given to the essay (e.g., out of 100).

strengths.description: Detailed description of the essay's strengths as identified by the AI.

weaknesses.description: Detailed description of the essay's weaknesses as identified by the AI.

improvementRoadmap.description: Actionable steps or a roadmap for the user to improve their essay writing skills.

modelAnswerOutline.description: A structural outline or summary of a model answer for the given essay prompt.

evaluationDate.description: Timestamp when the essay evaluation was performed.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is restricted to the owner, where `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. The document ID for 'profile' will be the user's Firebase Auth UID. The 'id' field within the document will also store this UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores study materials uploaded by a specific user. Access is restricted to the owner, where `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. The document includes the 'userId' field for authorization independence, matching the path parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this study material.

params.name: studyMaterialId
params.description: The unique identifier for a piece of study material.

definition.description: Stores assessments generated for a specific user. Access is restricted to the owner, where `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. The document includes the 'userId' field for authorization independence, matching the path parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who requested this assessment.

params.name: assessmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for an assessment.

definition.description: Stores individual questions belonging to a specific assessment. Access is restricted to the owner, where `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. Includes denormalized 'userId' from the parent 'Assessment' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent assessment.

params.name: assessmentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent assessment.

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a question.

definition.description: Records a user's attempt at an assessment. Access is restricted to the owner, where `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. The document includes the 'userId' field for authorization independence, matching the path parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who made this assessment attempt.

params.name: attemptId
params.description: The unique identifier for a user's assessment attempt.

definition.description: Stores a user's response to a specific question within an assessment attempt. Access is restricted to the owner, where `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. Includes denormalized 'userId' from the parent 'UserAssessmentAttempt' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent assessment attempt.

params.name: attemptId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent assessment attempt.

params.name: responseId
params.description: The unique identifier for a user's question response.

definition.description: Stores essays submitted by a user for evaluation. Access is restricted to the owner, where `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. The document includes the 'userId' field for authorization independence, matching the path parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who submitted this essay.

params.name: submittedEssayId
params.description: The unique identifier for a submitted essay.

definition.description: Stores AI evaluations for submitted essays. Access is restricted to the owner, where `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. Includes denormalized 'userId' from the parent 'SubmittedEssay' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent submitted essay.

params.name: submittedEssayId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent submitted essay.

params.name: evaluationId
params.description: The unique identifier for an essay evaluation.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to adhere strictly to the core design principles and strategy mandates, prioritizing security, scalability, and debuggability. All user-specific data is organized under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection, leveraging path-based ownership for robust and explicit authorization. This structure ensures that a user can only access their own data, significantly simplifying security rules.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** To achieve authorization independence, denormalization of the `userId` is a key strategy for subcollections. For `Question` documents (nested under `Assessment`), `EssayEvaluation` documents (nested under `SubmittedEssay`), and `UserQuestionResponse` documents (nested under `UserAssessmentAttempt`), the `userId` of the owning `UserProfile` will be explicitly stored within each document. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules to retrieve parent documents for authorization checks, enabling atomic operations and making rules more performant and easier to debug. For instance, a `Question` document will contain `questionText`, `assessmentId`, and `userId`, allowing a rule like `allow read: if request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId;` to function independently.

**Structural Segregation & QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** Every collection path is designed to have a homogeneous security posture. All data under `/users/{userId}` is strictly private to that `userId`. This directly addresses the QAP principle: `list` operations on any of these collections will inherently return only data belonging to the authenticated user (because `request.auth.uid` will be used to construct the path, e.g., `/users/{request.auth.uid}/study_materials`). There's no mixing of public, shared, or different ownership data within these user-specific collections, thus preventing the need for complex, inefficient rule-based filtering during reads. This design ensures that security rules are straightforward and declarative, primarily relying on `request.auth.uid == path.userId` or `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without performing any implicit filtering.
